Hi, I'm Ricky
June 20th Challenge Date

A personal challenge with a bigger purpose

I'm taking on the London to Brighton Bike Ride challenge to push myself to get healthier, but also to do something that matters beyond just me.

I'm supporting the British Heart Foundation, who do incredible work funding life saving research into heart and circulatory diseases. These conditions affect so many families across the UK, and every pound raised helps make a real difference.

This challenge is not just about the ride, it is about raising awareness and contributing to a cause that genuinely changes lives.

♥ From the heart

Why I'm doing this

♥ Ricky's message

First of all, thank you for visiting my page, I really appreciate it.

At the start of this year, I set myself two New Year's resolutions. The first was to get healthier and push myself physically. The second was to do something charitable and give back to a cause that matters. When I came across the London to Brighton Bike Ride, it felt like the perfect opportunity to combine both.

Since then, I have been putting in the work to prepare. That has meant getting out consistently, doing laps around Richmond Park, and pushing myself up steep hills to get ready for Brighton's tough terrain. It has definitely been a challenge, but that is exactly why I wanted to take it on.

I am doing all of this in support of the British Heart Foundation. Heart and circulatory diseases affect so many families across the UK, and the work they do in funding life saving research is genuinely incredible. Every breakthrough they make has the potential to save lives and give people more time with their loved ones.

If you are able to, I would really appreciate any support, whether that is a donation, sharing this page, or even just a message of encouragement. Every contribution, no matter the size, makes a real difference and helps me get closer to my target.

Thank you so much 🙌

🏃 The challenge

What I'm taking on

A personal fitness journey with a meaningful goal — and a lot of determination.

June 20th
The big day

54 miles from Clapham Common to Brighton — pushing myself for a great cause. If all goes well… I'll be celebrating with a very well-earned fish and chips at the finish. 🐟🍟

♥ Your impact

Where your money goes

Every donation — no matter the size — directly funds life-saving research and support.

🔬
£24

Two Hours of Research

Just £24 can fund two hours of research by an early career scientist working to defeat heart disease.

📉
50%

Deaths Halved

The BHF has helped halve the number of people dying from heart and circulatory diseases in the UK over the past decades.

🧬
100+

Research Projects

The BHF funds hundreds of research projects that are developing new treatments and cures for heart conditions.
